name: Team Topology
description: Map team types and interaction modes to optimize for fast flow and minimal cognitive load
systemPromptHint: Stream-aligned teams should be the majority — if most teams are platform or enabling, the topology is inverted. Collaboration mode should be temporary; if two teams are permanently collaborating, they should merge or define an x-as-a-service boundary. Every interaction adds cognitive load — teams with many connections are overloaded; reduce interactions rather than adding capacity. Platform teams exist to make stream-aligned teams autonomous, not to centralize control.

nodeTags.name: Stream-Aligned Team
nodeTags.description: Aligned to a single flow of work from a business domain segment

nodeTags.name: Platform Team
nodeTags.description: Provides internal services that reduce cognitive load for stream-aligned teams

nodeTags.name: Enabling Team
nodeTags.description: Helps other teams overcome obstacles and adopt new capabilities — temporary by nature

nodeTags.name: Complicated-Subsystem Team
nodeTags.description: Owns a subsystem requiring deep specialist knowledge that would overload other teams

derivedIndicators.name: Hub
derivedIndicators.description: Both provides and consumes interactions — watch for cognitive overload

derivedIndicators.name: Consumer
derivedIndicators.description: Only consumes services or facilitation from other teams
